llm_result.summary: The user is carrying high external emotional labor (being the 'strong one'), suppressing distress, and experiencing delayed emotional crashes, with fatigue/resentment from ongoing resilience expectations. Modifiable levers span social boundary-setting, help-seeking, emotion processing, self-compassion, sleep/biological regulation, mindfulness, social-support diversification, and self-monitoring. Implementing targeted BIO-PSYCHO-SOCIAL predictors across these domains can reduce chronic boundary overreach, improve help-seeking, lessen suppression and crash risk, and restore energy and authentic help-acceptance without pharmacological intervention.

solutions.solution_candidate: Structured boundary-setting program including decline scripts, scheduled boundary practice, and role-clarity tasks; delivered via brief coaching sessions with homework to practice saying no and limit-setting in low-stakes situations.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By legally and consistently establishing boundaries, the individual reduces external emotional demands (C01) and builds skills to decline when overwhelmed (C06), mitigating chronic overextension and subsequent resentment (C05).
solutions.measurement: Weekly boundary-assertion frequency, self-reported boundary efficacy (scale), and moments of boundary breach; caregiver/peer feedback on boundary behavior.
solutions.mapping_query: C06 boundary setting; decline scripts; reduce external demands (C01) via structured boundaries.

solutions.solution_candidate: Teach and practice assertive communication to request help when distressed, including short scripts, role-plays, and identification of safe people to ask.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved help-seeking self-efficacy and practical asking strategies reduce personal distress and external dependency load, decreasing suppression and later crashes (C02, C01).
solutions.measurement: Help-seeking self-efficacy scores, frequency of asking for help per week, perceived responsiveness from others.
solutions.mapping_query: C02 difficulty requesting help; improve help-seeking behaviors; impact on C01.

solutions.solution_candidate: Emotion expression protocol combining journaling, verbal disclosure practice, and emotion labeling to reduce suppression and promote processing of distress.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Reducing habitual suppression (C03) lowers the risk of delayed crashes (C04) by enabling timely processing and release of negative affect.
solutions.measurement: Frequency of emotional expression in safe settings, suppression scores, number of crash/flare episodes per month.
solutions.mapping_query: C03 emotion suppression; C04 delayed breakdowns; increase emotional processing.

solutions.solution_candidate: Compassion-focused exercises and self-compassion journaling to reframe fatigue from being 'the strong one' and reduce resentment and self-judgment.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhancing self-compassion reduces internal blame and resentment (C05) and supports healthier emotional regulation (C03), dampening the drive to suppress.
solutions.measurement: Self-compassion scale scores, burnout/resentment measures, mood ratings.
solutions.mapping_query: C05 role fatigue; resentment; C03 suppression via self-compassion.

solutions.solution_candidate: Implement a structured schedule for emotional labor, including a designated boundary window and a rotating peer-support buddy to share the load.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: By systematizing when and whom support is available, external demands become predictable and manageable, reducing chronic load and improving boundary maintenance (C01, C06).
solutions.measurement: Number of boundary breaches, average weekly requests, perceived workload.
solutions.mapping_query: C01 frequency of being sought; C06 boundary setting via scheduling.

solutions.solution_candidate: Sleep hygiene program with consistent bed/wake times, wind-down routine, light exposure management, and circadian alignment to support daytime regulation.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Improved sleep quality and regular circadian rhythms enhance emotional regulation, reducing suppression (C03) and likelihood of crashes after stress (C04).
solutions.measurement: Sleep duration/quality metrics (diary or PSQI), next-day mood/alertness.
solutions.mapping_query: C03 sleep; C04 crash risk linked to sleep.

solutions.solution_candidate: 8-week mindfulness or ACT-based program to cultivate nonjudgmental awareness, values-aligned action, and stress tolerance.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Enhanced awareness and acceptance of distress reduces avoidance/masking (C03) and reactive crashes (C04), supports healthier relationship with self (C05).
solutions.measurement: Perceived stress scale, mindfulness/acceptance measures, daily mood tracking.

solutions.solution_candidate: Develop and engage with a broader social support network (community groups, peer networks) to diversify sources of support beyond the primary respondent.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Broadening support reduces sole dependence on one person and lowers ongoing demands, mitigating resentment and overload (C01, C05).
solutions.measurement: Network size, perceived social support, frequency of supportive contacts.
solutions.mapping_query: C01 social support; C05 burden/resentment via network expansion.

solutions.solution_candidate: Daily self-monitoring (mood, energy, boundary events) with automated feedback; optional wearables for autonomic/cardiometabolic correlates to guide adjustments.
solutions.mechanism_rationale: Increased self-awareness and rapid feedback enable timely adjustments to boundary use, help-seeking, emotion processing, and energy management, affecting all six criteria.
solutions.measurement: Daily logs of mood, energy, boundary events, help-seeking instances; adherence to feedback.
solutions.mapping_query: All criteria; self-monitoring with feedback to drive multi-domain changes.
